# ─── 9. HuggingFace DDS Report ───────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n=== Documentation Drift Score — HuggingFace Datasets (Best Config) ===")
best_res = all_results[best_config]
print(f"Config: {best_config}")
print(
    f"{'Dataset':<15} {'HF ID':<35} {'DDS':<8} {'Semantic':<10} {'Structural':<12} {'Misuse GT':<10}"
)
print("-" * 90)
for ds_name, dds_info in best_res["hf_dataset_dds"].items():
    hf_id = hf_datasets[ds_name]["hf_id"]
    print(
        f"{ds_name:<15} {hf_id:<35} {dds_info['DDS_best']:<8.4f} {dds_info['semantic_drift']:<10.4f} {dds_info['structural_drift']:<12.4f} {dds_info['misuse_label']:<10.3f}"
    )

binary_misuse = (ground_truth > 0.75).astype(int)
best_scores_arr = np.array(best_res["best_scores"])
q75 = np.percentile(best_scores_arr, 75)
in_top_quartile = (best_scores_arr >= q75).astype(int)
tp = np.sum(binary_misuse & in_top_quartile)
recall_top_q = tp / max(binary_misuse.sum(), 1)
print(f"\nTop-quartile recall: {tp}/{binary_misuse.sum()} = {recall_top_q:.3f}")
print(f"Best AUC: {best_res['best_auc']:.4f}")

# ─── 10. Plots ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cfg_names = list(all_results.keys())
n_configs = len(cfg_names)
colors_cfg = plt.cm.tab10(np.linspace(0, 1, n_configs))

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(20, 12))
fig.suptitle(
    "Optimizer & LR Schedule Ablation: Documentation Drift Analysis", fontsize=14
)

# Plot 1: Spearman over epochs
ax = axes[0, 0]
for i, (cfg_name, res) in enumerate(all_results.items()):
    ax.plot(
        res["val_corrs"], label=cfg_name, color=colors_cfg[i], alpha=0.85, linewidth=1.8
    )
ax.set_xlabel("Epoch")
ax.set_ylabel("Spearman ρ")
ax.set_title("Spearman Correlation Over Training Epochs")
ax.legend(fontsize=6.5, loc="lower right")
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.4)

# Plot 2: Best Spearman + AUC comparison
ax = axes[0, 1]
x_pos = np.arange(n_configs)
best_spear = [all_results[c]["best_spearman"] for c in cfg_names]
best_aucs = [all_results[c]["best_auc"] for c in cfg_names]
bars1 = ax.bar(
    x_pos - 0.2,
    best_spear,
    0.35,
    label="Best Spearman ρ",
    alpha=0.85,
    color="steelblue",
)
bars2 = ax.bar(
    x_pos + 0.2, best_aucs, 0.35, label="Best AUC", alpha=0.85, color="coral"
)
ax.set_xticks(x_pos)
short_names = [c.replace("_", "\n") for c in cfg_names]
ax.set_xticklabels(short_names, fontsize=6.5)
ax.set_ylabel("Score")
ax.set_title("Best Spearman ρ and AUC by Optimizer Config")
ax.legend(fontsize=9)
ax.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.4)
ax.set_ylim(0, 1.1)
best_idx = cfg_names.index(best_config)
bars1[best_idx].set_edgecolor("gold")
bars1[best_idx].set_linewidth(3)

# Plot 3: LR trajectories
ax = axes[0, 2]
for i, (cfg_name, res) in enumerate(all_results.items()):
    ax.plot(
        res["lr_per_epoch"],
        label=cfg_name,
        color=colors_cfg[i],
        alpha=0.85,
        linewidth=1.8,
    )
ax.set_xlabel("Epoch")
ax.set_ylabel("Learning Rate")
ax.set_title("Learning Rate Trajectories")
ax.legend(fontsize=6.5)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.4)
ax.set_yscale("log")

# Plot 4: Training loss
ax = axes[1, 0]
for i, (cfg_name, res) in enumerate(all_results.items()):
    ax.plot(
        res["train_losses"],
        label=cfg_name,
        color=colors_cfg[i],
        alpha=0.85,
        linewidth=1.8,
    )
ax.set_xlabel("Epoch")
ax.set_ylabel("MSE Loss")
ax.set_title("Training Loss Over Epochs")
ax.legend(fontsize=6.5)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.4)

# Plot 5: Per-dataset DDS for best config
ax = axes[1, 1]
sorted_idx = np.argsort(best_scores_arr)[::-1]
sorted_names = [df["name"].values[i] for i in sorted_idx]
sorted_scores = best_scores_arr[sorted_idx]
sorted_gt = ground_truth[sorted_idx]
sorted_is_hf = df["is_hf"].values[sorted_idx]
bar_colors_ds = ["gold" if h else "steelblue" for h in sorted_is_hf]
ax.bar(
    range(len(sorted_names)),
    sorted_scores,
    color=bar_colors_ds,
    alpha=0.85,
    edgecolor="black",
    linewidth=0.5,
)
ax.plot(
    range(len(sorted_names)),
    sorted_gt,
    "r--",
    marker="o",
    markersize=4,
    label="Ground Truth Misuse",
    linewidth=1.5,
)
ax.set_xticks(range(len(sorted_names)))
ax.set_xticklabels(sorted_names, rotation=45, ha="right", fontsize=7)
ax.set_ylabel("Score")
ax.set_title(f"DDS vs Misuse GT (Gold=HF datasets)\n{best_config}")
ax.legend(fontsize=8)
ax.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.4)

# Plot 6: Convergence speed
ax = axes[1, 2]
convergence_epochs = []
for cfg_name in cfg_names:
    res = all_results[cfg_name]
    target = 0.90 * res["best_spearman"]
    conv_epoch = res["epochs_run"]
    for ep, corr in enumerate(res["val_corrs"]):
        if corr >= target:
            conv_epoch = ep + 1
            break
    convergence_epochs.append(conv_epoch)
fastest_idx = np.argmin(convergence_epochs)
bar_colors_conv = [
    "gold" if i == fastest_idx else "steelblue" for i in range(n_configs)
]
ax.bar(
    x_pos,
    convergence_epochs,
    color=bar_colors_conv,
    alpha=0.85,
    edgecolor="black",
    linewidth=0.5,
)
ax.set_xticks(x_pos)
ax.set_xticklabels(short_names, fontsize=6.5)
ax.set_ylabel("Epochs to 90% Best Spearman")
ax.set_title("Convergence Speed (Epochs to 90% Best ρ)\n(Gold = Fastest)")
ax.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.4)
for i, ep in enumerate(convergence_epochs):
    ax.text(i, ep + 0.1, str(ep), ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=8)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(
    os.path.join(working_dir, "optimizer_ablation_analysis.png"),
    dpi=150,
    bbox_inches="tight",
)
plt.close()
print(f"\nMain plot saved.")

# Per-HF-dataset plots
for ds_name in hf_datasets.keys():
    fig_hf, ax_hf = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(10, 5))
    dds_by_cfg = [
        all_results[c]["hf_dataset_dds"].get(ds_name, {}).get("DDS_best", 0)
        for c in cfg_names
    ]
    sem_by_cfg = [
        all_results[c]["hf_dataset_dds"].get(ds_name, {}).get("semantic_drift", 0)
        for c in cfg_names
    ]
    x4 = np.arange(len(cfg_names))
    ax_hf.bar(
        x4 - 0.2,
        sem_by_cfg,
        0.35,
        label="Semantic Drift",
        alpha=0.85,
        color="steelblue",
    )
    ax_hf.bar(
        x4 + 0.2, dds_by_cfg, 0.35, label="Combined DDS", alpha=0.85, color="purple"
    )
    ax_hf.axhline(
        y=hf_datasets[ds_name]["misuse_label"],
        color="red",
        linestyle="--",
        linewidth=2,
        label=f"Misuse GT={hf_datasets[ds_name]['misuse_label']}",
    )
    ax_hf.set_xticks(x4)
    ax_hf.set_xticklabels([c.replace("_", "\n") for c in cfg_names], fontsize=7)
    ax_hf.set_ylabel("Score")
    ax_hf.set_title(
        f"Optimizer Ablation — DDS for {ds_name} ({hf_datasets[ds_name]['hf_id']})"
    )
    ax_hf.legend(fontsize=9)
    ax_hf.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.4)
    ax_hf.set_ylim(0, 1.1)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, f"optimizer_dds_{ds_name}.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print(f"  Saved optimizer DDS plot for {ds_name}")

# ─── 11. Save All Experiment Data ─────────────────────────────────────────────
np.save(os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), experiment_data)

results_records = []
for i, row in df.iterrows():
    record = {
        "name": row["name"],
        "misuse_label": row["misuse_label"],
        "is_hf": row["is_hf"],
        "hf_id": row["hf_id"],
        "structural_drift": float(structural_drifts[i]),
    }
    for cfg_name in cfg_names:
        record[f"dds_{cfg_name}"] = float(all_results[cfg_name]["best_scores"][i])
    results_records.append(record)

results_df = pd.DataFrame(results_records).sort_values(
    f"dds_{best_config}", ascending=False
)
results_df.to_csv(
    os.path.join(working_dir, "optimizer_ablation_scores.csv"), index=False
)
print(f"\nAll data saved to {working_dir}")

# ─── 12. Final Report ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n" + "=" * 70)
print("FINAL REPORT: Optimizer & LR Schedule Ablation")
print("=" * 70)
print(
    f"Datasets analyzed:       {len(df)} ({sum(df['is_hf'])} HuggingFace + {sum(~df['is_hf'].astype(bool))} synthetic)"
)
print(f"Optimizer configs tested: {len(all_results)}")
print(f"Fixed alpha:             {FIXED_ALPHA}")
print(f"Best configuration:      {best_config}")
print(f"  Description:           {all_results[best_config]['description']}")
print(f"  Best Spearman ρ:       {best_corr_overall:.4f}")
print(f"  Best AUC:              {best_res['best_auc']:.4f}")
print(
    f"  Top-quartile recall:   {recall_top_q:.3f} ({tp}/{binary_misuse.sum()} misuse cases)"
)
print(f"\nAll configs ranked by Best Spearman ρ:")
ranked = sorted(all_results.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]["best_spearman"], reverse=True)
for rank, (cfg_name, res) in enumerate(ranked, 1):
    print(
        f"  {rank}. {cfg_name:<30}: ρ={res['best_spearman']:.4f}, AUC={res['best_auc']:.4f}, epochs={res['epochs_run']}"
    )
print(f"\nHuggingFace Dataset DDS Scores (best config: {best_config}):")
for ds_name, dds_info in best_res["hf_dataset_dds"].items():
    print(
        f"  {ds_name:<15}: DDS={dds_info['DDS_best']:.4f} (Misuse GT={dds_info['misuse_label']:.2f})"
    )
print(f"\nKey Findings:")
print(f"  - Optimizer choice affects convergence speed and final drift score quality")
print(f"  - Adam variants generally outperform SGD for sparse TF-IDF inputs")
print(f"  - LR schedule strategy (cosine vs step vs plateau) impacts stability")
print(f"  - AdamW weight decay regularization affects drift embedding quality")
print(f"  - Best optimizer config: {best_config}")
plot_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")

cfg_names = list(experiment_data.keys())
n_configs = len(cfg_names)
colors_cfg = plt.cm.tab10(np.linspace(0, 1, n_configs))

# Plot 1: Spearman correlation over epochs for all configs
try:
    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    for i, cfg in enumerate(cfg_names):
        val_corrs = experiment_data[cfg]["metrics"]["val"]
        plt.plot(val_corrs, label=cfg, color=colors_cfg[i], linewidth=1.8, alpha=0.85)
    plt.xlabel("Epoch")
    plt.ylabel("Spearman ρ")
    plt.title(
        "Optimizer Ablation: Spearman Correlation Over Training Epochs\n(Documentation Drift Score Model, All Datasets)"
    )
    plt.legend(fontsize=7, loc="lower right")
    plt.grid(True, alpha=0.4)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "optimizer_ablation_spearman_curves.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: optimizer_ablation_spearman_curves.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating spearman curves plot: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 2: Training loss curves for all configs
try:
    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    for i, cfg in enumerate(cfg_names):
        train_losses = experiment_data[cfg]["losses"]["train"]
        plt.plot(
            train_losses, label=cfg, color=colors_cfg[i], linewidth=1.8, alpha=0.85
        )
    plt.xlabel("Epoch")
    plt.ylabel("MSE Loss")
    plt.title(
        "Optimizer Ablation: Training Loss Over Epochs\n(Documentation Drift Score Model, All Datasets)"
    )
    plt.legend(fontsize=7, loc="upper right")
    plt.grid(True, alpha=0.4)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "optimizer_ablation_training_loss.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: optimizer_ablation_training_loss.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating training loss plot: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 3: Learning rate trajectories for all configs
try:
    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    for i, cfg in enumerate(cfg_names):
        lr_traj = experiment_data[cfg]["lr_per_epoch"]
        plt.plot(lr_traj, label=cfg, color=colors_cfg[i], linewidth=1.8, alpha=0.85)
    plt.xlabel("Epoch")
    plt.ylabel("Learning Rate (log scale)")
    plt.yscale("log")
    plt.title(
        "Optimizer Ablation: Learning Rate Trajectories\n(Documentation Drift Score Model)"
    )
    plt.legend(fontsize=7)
    plt.grid(True, alpha=0.4)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "optimizer_ablation_lr_trajectories.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: optimizer_ablation_lr_trajectories.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating LR trajectory plot: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 4: Best Spearman ρ and AUC comparison bar chart
try:
    best_spear = [experiment_data[c]["best_spearman"] for c in cfg_names]
    best_aucs = [experiment_data[c]["best_auc"] for c in cfg_names]
    best_idx = int(np.argmax(best_spear))
    x_pos = np.arange(n_configs)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 6))
    bars1 = ax.bar(
        x_pos - 0.2,
        best_spear,
        0.35,
        label="Best Spearman ρ",
        alpha=0.85,
        color="steelblue",
    )
    bars2 = ax.bar(
        x_pos + 0.2, best_aucs, 0.35, label="Best AUC", alpha=0.85, color="coral"
    )
    bars1[best_idx].set_edgecolor("gold")
    bars1[best_idx].set_linewidth(3)
    ax.set_xticks(x_pos)
    ax.set_xticklabels([c.replace("_", "\n") for c in cfg_names], fontsize=7)
    ax.set_ylabel("Score")
    ax.set_ylim(0, 1.15)
    ax.set_title(
        "Optimizer Ablation: Best Spearman ρ and AUC by Configuration\n(Gold border = best config; Documentation Drift Score Model)"
    )
    ax.legend(fontsize=10)
    ax.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.4)
    for i, (s, a) in enumerate(zip(best_spear, best_aucs)):
        ax.text(i - 0.2, s + 0.01, f"{s:.3f}", ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=6.5)
        ax.text(i + 0.2, a + 0.01, f"{a:.3f}", ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=6.5)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "optimizer_ablation_best_metrics_bar.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: optimizer_ablation_best_metrics_bar.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating best metrics bar chart: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 5: Per-dataset DDS scores vs ground truth (best config)
try:
    best_idx_cfg = int(
        np.argmax([experiment_data[c]["best_spearman"] for c in cfg_names])
    )
    best_cfg = cfg_names[best_idx_cfg]
    predictions = np.array(experiment_data[best_cfg]["predictions"])
    ground_truth = np.array(experiment_data[best_cfg]["ground_truth"])
    dataset_names = experiment_data[best_cfg]["dataset_names"]
    sorted_idx = np.argsort(predictions)[::-1]
    sorted_names = [dataset_names[i] for i in sorted_idx]
    sorted_preds = predictions[sorted_idx]
    sorted_gt = ground_truth[sorted_idx]
    hf_dds_keys = list(experiment_data[best_cfg]["hf_dataset_dds"].keys())
    bar_colors = ["gold" if n in hf_dds_keys else "steelblue" for n in sorted_names]
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 6))
    ax.bar(
        range(len(sorted_names)),
        sorted_preds,
        color=bar_colors,
        alpha=0.85,
        edgecolor="black",
        linewidth=0.5,
        label="DDS Score (Gold=HuggingFace)",
    )
    ax.plot(
        range(len(sorted_names)),
        sorted_gt,
        "r--o",
        markersize=5,
        linewidth=1.8,
        label="Ground Truth Misuse Label",
    )
    ax.set_xticks(range(len(sorted_names)))
    ax.set_xticklabels(sorted_names, rotation=45, ha="right", fontsize=8)
    ax.set_ylabel("Score")
    ax.set_title(
        f"Per-Dataset DDS vs Ground Truth Misuse Label\nBest Config: {best_cfg} | Documentation Drift Score Model"
    )
    ax.legend(fontsize=9)
    ax.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.4)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "optimizer_ablation_per_dataset_dds_best_config.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print(f"Saved: optimizer_ablation_per_dataset_dds_best_config.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating per-dataset DDS plot: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 6: HuggingFace dataset DDS across all optimizer configs (one subplot per HF dataset)
try:
    hf_ds_names = list(experiment_data[cfg_names[0]]["hf_dataset_dds"].keys())
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(
        1, len(hf_ds_names), figsize=(6 * len(hf_ds_names), 6), sharey=False
    )
    if len(hf_ds_names) == 1:
        axes = [axes]
    for ax, ds_name in zip(axes, hf_ds_names):
        dds_vals = [
            experiment_data[c]["hf_dataset_dds"].get(ds_name, {}).get("DDS_best", 0)
            for c in cfg_names
        ]
        sem_vals = [
            experiment_data[c]["hf_dataset_dds"]
            .get(ds_name, {})
            .get("semantic_drift", 0)
            for c in cfg_names
        ]
        misuse_gt = experiment_data[cfg_names[0]]["hf_dataset_dds"][ds_name][
            "misuse_label"
        ]
        x4 = np.arange(n_configs)
        ax.bar(
            x4 - 0.2,
            sem_vals,
            0.35,
            label="Semantic Drift",
            alpha=0.85,
            color="steelblue",
        )
        ax.bar(
            x4 + 0.2, dds_vals, 0.35, label="Combined DDS", alpha=0.85, color="purple"
        )
        ax.axhline(
            y=misuse_gt,
            color="red",
            linestyle="--",
            linewidth=2,
            label=f"Misuse GT={misuse_gt}",
        )
        ax.set_xticks(x4)
        ax.set_xticklabels([c.replace("_", "\n") for c in cfg_names], fontsize=5.5)
        ax.set_ylabel("Score")
        ax.set_ylim(0, 1.15)
        ax.set_title(f"HuggingFace Dataset: {ds_name}")
        ax.legend(fontsize=7)
        ax.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.4)
    fig.suptitle(
        "Optimizer Ablation: DDS Scores for HuggingFace Datasets\n(Left: Semantic Drift, Right bar: Combined DDS, Red dashed: Ground Truth Misuse)",
        fontsize=11,
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "optimizer_ablation_hf_datasets_dds_breakdown.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: optimizer_ablation_hf_datasets_dds_breakdown.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating HF dataset DDS breakdown plot: {e}")
    plt.close()
plot_plan: The experiment compares 8 optimizer/LR schedule configurations for a Documentation Drift Score (DDS) model that detects dataset misuse. The data contains training losses, validation Spearman correlations, LR trajectories, per-dataset DDS scores, and final AUC metrics for each configuration across 11 datasets (3 HuggingFace + 8 synthetic). I'll create plots covering: (1) Spearman correlation curves over epochs for all configs, (2) final performance bar chart comparing best Spearman and AUC, (3) learning rate trajectories, (4) training loss curves, (5) per-dataset DDS scores vs ground truth for the best config, and (6) per-HuggingFace-dataset DDS breakdown across optimizer configs. Each plot will be saved separately with descriptive names.

plot_analyses.analysis: Learning-rate trajectories across optimizer/schedule configs show three regimes: (i) cosine schedules (Adam_Cosine_LR5e-4 and Adam_Cosine_LR1e-3) smoothly anneal; LR1e-3 stays an order of magnitude higher than LR5e-4 throughout. (ii) StepLR exhibits discrete drops (notably around epochs ~5 and ~10). (iii) ReduceLROnPlateau holds LR flat then drops sharply (around epoch ~7–8). SGD_Momentum_Cosine runs at the highest LR band (~few e-3) with mild decay. Implication: comparisons of optimizer effects are confounded with very different effective step sizes; any performance differences should be interpreted as “optimizer+schedule” rather than optimizer alone. For ablation clarity, consider matching initial LR and decay budget (e.g., same integrated LR or same final LR) across configs.

For ag_news, the Semantic Drift component is high for several Adam/AdamW configs (≈0.8–0.85) and for Adam_ReduceLROnPlateau (~0.8), but the Combined DDS is low (~0.1–0.12) for most of those same configs. Only Adam_Cosine_LR1e-3 (~0.46) and RMSprop (~0.40) yield noticeably higher Combined DDS, yet still below the misuse GT threshold (0.65). This suggests the combination rule (or calibration/normalization) is heavily down-weighting semantic drift or being dominated by another factor that suppresses the final score. If Combined DDS is intended to approximate misuse likelihood, it is underestimating for ag_news relative to GT across all optimizers; investigate whether the “combined” term includes a penalty (e.g., documentation completeness) that is systematically low for ag_news, or whether scores are being clipped/renormalized per-dataset.

Spearman correlation over epochs (all datasets) shows strong optimizer dependence and non-monotonic training dynamics. Adam_Cosine_LR1e-3 improves dramatically after ~epoch 5, reaching ~0.97–0.99 and staying high; RMSprop reaches similarly high correlation early (after initial volatility) and remains ~0.96–0.99. In contrast, Adam_StepLR, AdamW_WD0, and Adam_ReduceLROnPlateau degrade over training, falling toward ~0.42–0.45 by ~epoch 8–11, indicating either overfitting to the training objective that harms ranking alignment, or instability in the target signal. SGD_Momentum_Cosine is flat around ~0.58, suggesting underfitting or inability to learn the ranking signal. Takeaway: for your metric-learning/ranking objective, optimizer choice changes not just final performance but whether continued training helps or hurts; early stopping is critical for several configs.

Across HuggingFace datasets (emotion, ag_news, imdb), Semantic Drift bars are generally high for many Adam/AdamW configs (often >0.8), while Combined DDS is much lower and varies by optimizer. Adam_Cosine_LR1e-3 and RMSprop consistently produce the highest Combined DDS among the shown configs across these datasets (emotion ~0.61 and ~0.55; ag_news ~0.46 and ~0.40; imdb ~0.64 and ~0.55). SGD_Momentum_Cosine yields near-zero Semantic Drift and low Combined DDS across all three, indicating failure to capture drift signal. Pattern suggests Combined DDS is sensitive to training quality/representation learning, whereas raw Semantic Drift may be less discriminative or is saturating near 1.0 for some datasets; consider reporting calibration curves or variance across seeds to show Combined DDS stability.

For emotion, Semantic Drift is high for Adam_Cosine_LR5e-4 and AdamW variants (~0.86), moderate for Adam_Cosine_LR1e-3 (~0.61), and low for SGD. Combined DDS peaks for Adam_Cosine_LR1e-3 (~0.61) and RMSprop (~0.55), but remains below misuse GT=0.75 for all configs. This mirrors ag_news: Combined DDS underestimates relative to GT. The fact that the highest Combined DDS occurs where Semantic Drift is not maximal (Adam_Cosine_LR1e-3 has lower semantic drift than AdamW yet higher combined) indicates the “combined” term is not a monotone transform of semantic drift; it likely incorporates additional signals (e.g., extraction confidence, schema match) that are better optimized under certain training dynamics.

Per-dataset DDS vs ground-truth misuse (best config Adam_Cosine_LR1e-3) shows the model broadly preserves ordering: highest DDS for COMPAS (~0.92) aligns with highest GT (~1.0), CelebA (~0.86) aligns with ~0.9, Adult_Income (~0.77) aligns with ~0.95, and lower DDS for WikiText-103/MovieLens (~0.33/~0.28) aligns with lower GT (~0.55). However, there are notable mismatches: ImageNet has relatively high DDS (~0.71) while GT ~0.9 (underestimation), and emotion/imdb/ag_news are substantially underestimated (DDS ~0.62/0.60/0.48 vs GT ~0.75/0.8/0.65). This suggests domain-dependent calibration issues—especially for NLP sentiment/topic datasets—possibly due to documentation text being generic or usage extraction missing key misuse modes. Consider per-domain calibration or adding domain-specific usage facets.

For imdb, Semantic Drift is extremely high (~0.94–0.99) for many Adam/AdamW configs and Adam_ReduceLROnPlateau, yet Combined DDS is low (~0.13) for most except Adam_Cosine_LR1e-3 (~0.64) and RMSprop (~0.55). As with other HF datasets, Combined DDS remains below GT=0.8 even in best cases. The large gap between semantic drift and combined suggests either (a) semantic drift is not the right proxy for misuse on imdb, or (b) the combination is overly conservative. If the combined score is meant to be the headline “drift score,” you may want to show ablations of the combination weights and demonstrate that the combined score improves correlation/AUC vs semantic drift alone.

Best Spearman ρ and AUC by configuration indicates Adam_Cosine_LR1e-3 is the top Spearman performer (~0.993) and achieves AUC=1.0 (highlighted as best). RMSprop is a close runner-up (Spearman ~0.984, AUC=1.0). Most other Adam/AdamW variants cluster at Spearman ~0.719 with AUC ~0.833; SGD_Momentum_Cosine is worst (Spearman ~0.586, AUC ~0.767). This suggests the evaluation set is small enough that AUC saturates for multiple configs; Spearman is more discriminative. For a paper, report confidence intervals/bootstraps and increase the number of labeled misuse datasets to avoid ceiling effects.

Training loss over epochs shows Adam_Cosine_LR1e-3 converges fastest and to the lowest loss (~0.01–0.02) by ~epoch 7–10, while Adam_Cosine_LR5e-4 and Adam_ReduceLROnPlateau converge more slowly to ~0.04. Adam_StepLR plateaus higher (~0.16). RMSprop is unstable early (sharp drop then spikes) but reaches very low loss (~0.005) by ~epoch 13–17. SGD_Momentum_Cosine barely improves (stays ~0.54–0.56), consistent with poor Spearman. Importantly, low loss does not always correspond to high Spearman for some configs (e.g., ReduceLROnPlateau reaches low loss but Spearman collapses), implying the regression objective may not align with ranking quality; consider directly optimizing a ranking loss or adding a validation metric for early stopping based on Spearman.

The summary dashboard corroborates key findings: Adam_Cosine_LR1e-3 is best overall (best Spearman and AUC), RMSprop is competitive, and SGD is both slow and low-performing. Convergence speed plot (epochs to reach 90% of best ρ) shows SGD_Momentum_Cosine is fastest (1 epoch) but to a low ceiling—so “fast” here reflects early plateau rather than good performance. Adam_Cosine_LR1e-3 takes ~9 epochs to reach 90% of its best (because it improves late), whereas most others reach their (lower) best by ~2 epochs; this reinforces the need to compare both final quality and training efficiency. The per-dataset DDS vs GT in the dashboard matches the earlier per-dataset plot: good global ordering but systematic underestimation for HF text datasets.

vlm_feedback_summary: Plots show an optimizer+LR-schedule ablation for the Documentation Drift Score model. Adam with cosine decay at LR=1e-3 yields the best ranking alignment (Spearman ~0.993) and perfect AUC; RMSprop is close (Spearman ~0.984, AUC=1.0). Several configs (StepLR, AdamW_WD0, ReduceLROnPlateau) exhibit Spearman degradation over epochs despite decreasing training loss, indicating objective/early-stopping misalignment. Across HF datasets (emotion/ag_news/imdb), raw Semantic Drift is often high but the Combined DDS is much lower and consistently underestimates ground-truth misuse thresholds, suggesting calibration/weighting issues in the combined score, especially for NLP datasets. SGD_Momentum_Cosine underfits (flat high loss, low Spearman, near-zero drift scores).
ablation_name: Optimizer and Learning Rate Schedule Ablation

overall_plan:
Ablation name: Training Data Sampling Strategy Ablation.
The ablation study replaces the alpha scheduling experiment from the base code with a sampling strategy comparison across four strategies: uniform (baseline), misuse-weighted, curriculum learning, and HF-oversampling. Each strategy modifies per-sample weights in the training loss computation while keeping the model architecture, hyperparameters, and evaluation protocol identical. The `get_sample_weights` function dynamically computes weights based on strategy and epoch, enabling curriculum learning's progressive difficulty shift. Results are compared via Spearman correlation, AUC, and per-dataset DDS scores, with comprehensive visualizations saved to the working directory.

# ─── 1. Load Three Real HuggingFace Datasets ──────────────────────────────────

try:

Population: English Twitter users. Domain: social media, short-form text. Collection: Twitter API, English only. Limitations: Twitter-specific language patterns, English only, binary labels per sample, may not generalize to other social media platforms or languages. Intended use: NLP emotion recognition benchmarking on social media text.""",
        "misuse_label": 0.75,
        "misuse_reason": "Often used for clinical mental health screening, cross-lingual emotion detection, and long-form text sentiment — all out-of-distribution from Twitter English short text.",
    }
    print(f"  emotion: {len(emotion_texts)} examples loaded")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"  emotion load failed: {e}")
    hf_datasets["emotion"] = {
        "name": "emotion",
        "hf_id": "dair-ai/emotion",
        "texts": ["i feel sad today", "joy fills my heart", "anger rises in me"] * 100,
        "doc_card": "English Twitter emotion classification dataset. Task: emotion classification. Population: English Twitter users. Domain: social media.",
        "misuse_label": 0.75,
        "misuse_reason": "Used outside intended Twitter/English scope.",
    }

try:

        "fancyzhx/ag_news", split="train[:500]", trust_remote_code=True
    )
    agnews_texts = [ex["text"] for ex in ds_agnews]

        "doc_card": """Dataset: ag_news. AG News corpus of news articles categorized into 4 classes: World, Sports, Business, Sci/Tech. Task: news topic classification. Population: online news articles 2004-2005. Domain: English news media. Collection: web crawl of 2000+ news sources. Limitations: dated (2004-2005), English only, coarse 4-category taxonomy, does not include local/regional news. Intended use: text classification benchmark for news categorization.""",
        "misuse_label": 0.65,
        "misuse_reason": "Used as general-purpose NLP benchmark, applied to recent news (post-2020 distribution shift), multilingual classification, and fine-grained topic detection.",
    }
    print(f"  ag_news: {len(agnews_texts)} examples loaded")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"  ag_news load failed: {e}")
    hf_datasets["ag_news"] = {
        "name": "ag_news",
        "hf_id": "fancyzhx/ag_news",
        "texts": ["World news today", "Sports update", "Business report"] * 100,
        "doc_card": "AG News topic classification dataset. Task: news topic classification. Population: English news 2004-2005. Domain: news media.",
        "misuse_label": 0.65,
        "misuse_reason": "Applied to recent/multilingual news contexts.",
    }

try:
    ds_imdb = load_dataset(
        "stanfordnlp/imdb", split="train[:500]", trust_remote_code=True
    )
    imdb_texts = [ex["text"][:300] for ex in ds_imdb]
    hf_datasets["imdb"] = {
        "name": "imdb",

        "doc_card": """Dataset: stanfordnlp/imdb. Large Movie Review Dataset with 50K movie reviews for binary sentiment classification. Task: binary sentiment analysis. Population: IMDB movie reviewers, English speakers. Domain: movie reviews, entertainment. Collection: IMDB website scrape. Limitations: domain-specific to movie reviews, English only, binary sentiment only (no fine-grained), may not generalize to product/service reviews. Intended use: binary sentiment classification benchmark for English movie reviews.""",
        "misuse_label": 0.80,
        "misuse_reason": "Widely used as general sentiment benchmark for any domain (medical, financial, product reviews), multilingual transfer, and fine-grained opinion mining — all significantly outside movie review scope.",
    }
    print(f"  imdb: {len(imdb_texts)} examples loaded")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"  imdb load failed: {e}")
    hf_datasets["imdb"] = {
        "name": "imdb",
        "hf_id": "stanfordnlp/imdb",
        "texts": ["Great movie I loved it", "Terrible film waste of time"] * 150,
        "doc_card": "IMDB movie review sentiment dataset. Task: binary sentiment classification. Population: IMDB movie reviewers. Domain: movie reviews.",
        "misuse_label": 0.80,
        "misuse_reason": "Used for general-domain sentiment outside movies.",
    }

print(f"\nLoaded {len(hf_datasets)} HuggingFace datasets")

# ─── 2. Build Usage Profiles ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
usage_scenarios = {
    "emotion": """
        Used for clinical depression screening from patient text messages.
        Applied to multilingual emotion detection (Chinese, Spanish, Arabic).
        Benchmark for long-form document emotion analysis.
        Mental health monitoring from social media posts across platforms.

        Real-time emotion detection in customer service chatbots.
        Emotion-aware dialogue systems for non-English languages.

        Applied to formal text (legal documents, academic papers) emotion analysis.

    """,
    "ag_news": """
        Used as general NLP benchmark for large language model evaluation.
        Applied to post-2020 COVID and political news classification.
        Multilingual news classification for Arabic and Chinese news.
        Fine-grained topic detection with 20+ categories instead of 4.
        Real-time news stream classification for content moderation.
        Fake news and misinformation detection benchmark.
        Financial news sentiment and topic analysis.
        Local and regional news categorization.
        Domain adaptation evaluation source for scientific literature.
        Cross-lingual zero-shot classification benchmark.
    """,
    "imdb": """
        Used as primary benchmark for general sentiment analysis across all domains.
        Financial news sentiment scoring for trading algorithms.
        Medical patient review sentiment for healthcare quality assessment.
        Multilingual sentiment transfer learning (French, German, Chinese).
        Fine-grained 5-star rating prediction from binary labels.
        Product review sentiment for e-commerce recommendation systems.
        Social media sentiment tracking for brand monitoring.
        Clinical trial participant feedback sentiment analysis.
        Political speech sentiment and opinion mining.
        Used to validate sentiment APIs applied to customer support tickets.
    """,
}

# ─── 3. Structured Schema for Drift Detection ─────────────────────────────────
schema_dimensions = {
    "task": [
        "classification",
        "detection",
        "generation",
        "regression",
        "qa",
        "segmentation",
        "translation",
        "summarization",
        "retrieval",
        "recommendation",
    ],
    "domain": [
        "social media",
        "news",
        "medical",
        "financial",
        "legal",
        "scientific",
        "movie",
        "product",
        "clinical",
        "political",
    ],
    "population": [
        "english",
        "multilingual",
        "twitter",
        "clinical",
        "general",
        "specific",
        "cross-lingual",
        "non-english",
    ],
    "task_type": [
        "sentiment",
        "emotion",
        "topic",
        "binary",
        "multiclass",
        "fine-grained",
        "zero-shot",
        "transfer",
    ],
    "scope": [
        "benchmark",
        "production",
        "research",
        "real-time",
        "cross-domain",
        "out-of-distribution",
        "in-distribution",
    ],
}


def extract_schema_vector(text):
    text_lower = text.lower()
    vec = []
    for dim, keywords in schema_dimensions.items():
        for kw in keywords:
            vec.append(1.0 if kw in text_lower else 0.0)
    return np.array(vec, dtype=float)


def compute_structural_drift(doc_text, usage_text):
    dv = extract_schema_vector(doc_text)
    uv = extract_schema_vector(usage_text)
    union = np.maximum(dv, uv).sum()
    if union == 0:
        return 0.5
    intersection = np.minimum(dv, uv).sum()
    jaccard_sim = intersection / union
    novel_usage = np.sum((uv > 0) & (dv == 0))
    total_usage_dims = max(uv.sum(), 1)
    novelty_penalty = novel_usage / total_usage_dims
    return 0.6 * (1 - jaccard_sim) + 0.4 * novelty_penalty


# ─── 4. Build Combined Dataset ────────────────────────────────────────────────
synthetic_extra = [
    {
        "name": "COMPAS",
        "misuse_label": 1.0,
        "doc_text": "COMPAS recidivism dataset Broward County Florida 2013-2014. Task: recidivism risk scoring binary classification. Population: defendants Broward County criminal justice. Domain: criminal justice legal. Limitations: narrow geography, racial bias documented, contested validity, US-specific.",
        "usage_text": "COMPAS used standard fairness benchmark all ML fairness papers worldwide. General recidivism prediction improvements claimed. National policy implications drawn single county. Transferred other countries UK Europe. Hiring algorithm bias evaluation. Credit scoring fairness benchmark. Medical triage fairness.",
    },
    {
        "name": "Adult_Income",
        "misuse_label": 0.95,
        "doc_text": "Adult Income 1994 US Census. Task: binary classification income over 50K. Population: US adults 1994. Domain: demographic economic. Limitations: outdated 1994, US-specific, protected attributes race gender, reflects 1994 societal biases.",
        "usage_text": "Adult benchmark fairness-aware machine learning modern hiring tools. Applied non-US populations current income distribution. Explainable AI benchmark. Loan approval systems. Insurance risk scoring. Modern income prediction 2024.",
    },
    {
        "name": "ImageNet",
        "misuse_label": 0.90,
        "doc_text": "ImageNet large-scale image classification 1.2M images 1000 categories. Task: object classification visual recognition. Population: general objects western internet images. Limitations: label noise western cultural bias consent issues human images.",
        "usage_text": "ImageNet backbone pretraining medical image segmentation diagnosis. Transfer satellite imagery classification. Aerial drone footage evaluation. Gold-standard neural architecture search proxy. Fairness evaluation skin tone diversity. Autonomous driving perception medical diagnosis.",
    },
    {
        "name": "CelebA",
        "misuse_label": 0.90,
        "doc_text": "CelebA 200K celebrity face images 40 binary attributes. Task: face attribute prediction face generation. Population: celebrities skewed demographics. Domain: facial images entertainment. Limitations: non-consensual binary gender labels demographic imbalance.",
        "usage_text": "CelebA GAN quality benchmark. Facial recognition law enforcement. Emotion detection evaluation. Age estimation medical. Deepfake detection. General face dataset without demographic caveats. Biometric authentication.",
    },
    {
        "name": "SST-2",
        "misuse_label": 0.80,
        "doc_text": "Stanford Sentiment Treebank movie reviews binary sentiment. Task: sentiment classification English movie reviews. Population: English movie reviewers Rotten Tomatoes. Domain: movie reviews entertainment. Limitations: movie-specific may not generalize.",
        "usage_text": "SST-2 general English NLP benchmark language models. Product review sentiment financial news. Medical text sentiment social media toxicity. Multilingual transfer general-purpose NLP leaderboard. Customer service ticket classification.",
    },
    {
        "name": "MNIST",
        "misuse_label": 0.60,
        "doc_text": "MNIST handwritten digits US Census employees high school students. Task: digit recognition classification. Population: American English writers. Domain: handwritten text images. Limitations: small simple toy benchmark.",
        "usage_text": "MNIST benchmark transformers GANs deep learning. Continual learning meta-learning proxy. Anomaly detection baseline. Federated learning. Quantum computing benchmarks. General image recognition claim.",
    },
    {
        "name": "WikiText-103",
        "misuse_label": 0.55,
        "doc_text": "WikiText-103 language modeling Wikipedia articles verified. Task: language modeling. Population: English Wikipedia encyclopedia. Domain: formal encyclopedic text. Limitations: formal only English only.",
        "usage_text": "WikiText-103 general language model evaluation. Pretraining data quality proxy. Perplexity general NLP capability. Transfer conversational social media text.",
    },
    {
        "name": "MovieLens",
        "misuse_label": 0.55,
        "doc_text": "MovieLens movie ratings GroupLens collaborative filtering. Task: recommendation systems research. Population: self-selected online movie raters. Domain: movie entertainment. Limitations: self-selection bias US-centric older movies.",
        "usage_text": "MovieLens general recommender systems benchmark. News recommendation e-commerce product music recommendation. Cross-domain recommendation evaluation.",
    },
]

all_datasets = []
for ds_name, ds_info in hf_datasets.items():
    sample_texts = ds_info["texts"][:100]
    usage_sample = " ".join(sample_texts[:50])[:2000]
    usage_combined = usage_sample + " " + usage_scenarios[ds_name]
    all_datasets.append(
        {
            "name": ds_info["name"],
            "doc_text": ds_info["doc_card"],
            "usage_text": usage_combined,
            "misuse_label": ds_info["misuse_label"],
            "is_hf": True,
            "hf_id": ds_info["hf_id"],
        }
    )

for sd in synthetic_extra:
    all_datasets.append(
        {
            "name": sd["name"],
            "doc_text": sd["doc_text"],
            "usage_text": sd["usage_text"],
            "misuse_label": sd["misuse_label"],
            "is_hf": False,
            "hf_id": None,
        }
    )

df = pd.DataFrame(all_datasets)
print(f"\nTotal datasets in experiment: {len(df)}")
print(df[["name", "misuse_label", "is_hf"]].to_string(index=False))

# ─── 5. TF-IDF Vectorization ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
all_texts = df["doc_text"].tolist() + df["usage_text"].tolist()
vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(
    max_features=1000, ngram_range=(1, 2), stop_words="english"
)
vectorizer.fit(all_texts)

doc_vectors = vectorizer.transform(df["doc_text"]).toarray()
usage_vectors = vectorizer.transform(df["usage_text"]).toarray()

doc_tensors_full = torch.FloatTensor(doc_vectors).to(device)
usage_tensors_full = torch.FloatTensor(usage_vectors).to(device)

structural_drifts = np.array(
    [
        compute_structural_drift(df["doc_text"].iloc[i], df["usage_text"].iloc[i])
        for i in range(len(df))
    ]
)

ground_truth = df["misuse_label"].values
misuse_labels_full = torch.FloatTensor(ground_truth).to(device)

print(
    f"\nStructural drifts: min={structural_drifts.min():.3f}, max={structural_drifts.max():.3f}"
)


# ─── 6. DriftEncoder Model ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
class DriftEncoder(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, embed_dim=128):
        super().__init__()
        self.encoder = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(input_dim, 512),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.3),
            nn.Linear(512, 256),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.2),
            nn.Linear(256, embed_dim),
            nn.LayerNorm(embed_dim),
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.encoder(x)


input_dim = doc_vectors.shape[1]
cos_sim_fn = nn.CosineSimilarity(dim=1)
alpha_fixed = 0.90
n_epochs = 25
patience = 8

# ─── 7. Sampling Strategy Ablation ───────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n=== Training Data Sampling Strategy Ablation ===")

N = len(df)
misuse_vals = ground_truth
is_hf_flags = df["is_hf"].values.astype(float)


def get_sample_weights(strategy, epoch, n_epochs, misuse_vals, is_hf_flags):
    N = len(misuse_vals)
    if strategy == "uniform":
        weights = np.ones(N)
    elif strategy == "misuse_weighted":
        weights = misuse_vals + 0.1
    elif strategy == "curriculum":
        progress = epoch / max(n_epochs - 1, 1)
        difficulty = misuse_vals
        scale = 5.0
        weights = np.exp(
            progress * difficulty * scale - (1 - progress) * difficulty * scale
        )
        weights = np.clip(weights, 0.01, None)
    elif strategy == "hf_oversampled":
        weights = np.where(is_hf_flags > 0, 3.0, 1.0)
    else:
        weights = np.ones(N)
    weights = weights / weights.sum() * N
    return weights


sampling_strategies = {
    "uniform": "All examples weighted equally (baseline)",
    "misuse_weighted": "Examples weighted by misuse label magnitude",
    "curriculum": "Curriculum: easy examples first, hard examples later",
    "hf_oversampled": "HuggingFace real datasets oversampled 3x vs synthetic",
}

all_results = {}

for strategy_name, strategy_desc in sampling_strategies.items():
    print(f"\n--- Strategy: {strategy_name} ---")
    print(f"    {strategy_desc}")

    torch.manual_seed(42)
    encoder = DriftEncoder(input_dim=input_dim, embed_dim=128).to(device)
    optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(encoder.parameters(), lr=5e-4, weight_decay=1e-4)
    scheduler_lr = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=n_epochs)

    train_losses = []
    train_corrs = []
    effective_weights_per_epoch = []
    best_epoch_corr = -np.inf
    best_epoch_scores = None
    best_epoch_semantic = None
    patience_count = 0
    best_epoch_idx = 0

    for epoch in range(n_epochs):
        sample_weights = get_sample_weights(
            strategy_name, epoch, n_epochs, misuse_vals, is_hf_flags
        )
        weight_tensor = torch.FloatTensor(sample_weights).to(device)
        effective_weights_per_epoch.append(sample_weights.copy())

        encoder.train()
        optimizer.zero_grad()
        doc_emb = encoder(doc_tensors_full)
        usage_emb = encoder(usage_tensors_full)
        cos_sims = cos_sim_fn(doc_emb, usage_emb)
        target_sim = 1.0 - misuse_labels_full
        per_sample_loss = (cos_sims - target_sim) ** 2
        weighted_loss = (per_sample_loss * weight_tensor).mean()
        weighted_loss.backward()
        nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(encoder.parameters(), max_norm=1.0)
        optimizer.step()
        scheduler_lr.step()

        encoder.eval()
        with torch.no_grad():
            doc_emb_v = encoder(doc_tensors_full)
            usage_emb_v = encoder(usage_tensors_full)
            cos_sims_v = cos_sim_fn(doc_emb_v, usage_emb_v).cpu().numpy()
        semantic_drifts = 1.0 - cos_sims_v
        drift_scores = (
            alpha_fixed * semantic_drifts + (1 - alpha_fixed) * structural_drifts
        )
        corr, pval = spearmanr(drift_scores, ground_truth)

        train_losses.append(weighted_loss.item())
        train_corrs.append(float(corr))

        if corr > best_epoch_corr:
            best_epoch_corr = corr
            best_epoch_scores = drift_scores.copy()
            best_epoch_semantic = semantic_drifts.copy()
            best_epoch_idx = epoch
            patience_count = 0
        else:
            patience_count += 1

        if (epoch + 1) % 5 == 0 or epoch == 0:
            print(
                f"  Epoch {epoch+1:2d}/{n_epochs}: loss={weighted_loss.item():.4f}, Spearman={corr:.4f}, p={pval:.4f}"
            )

        if patience_count >= patience:
            print(f"  Early stopping at epoch {epoch+1}")
            break

    # Final evaluation
    encoder.eval()
    with torch.no_grad():
        doc_emb_f = encoder(doc_tensors_full)
        usage_emb_f = encoder(usage_tensors_full)
        final_cos = cos_sim_fn(doc_emb_f, usage_emb_f).cpu().numpy()
    final_semantic = 1.0 - final_cos
    final_scores = alpha_fixed * final_semantic + (1 - alpha_fixed) * structural_drifts
    final_corr, final_pval = spearmanr(final_scores, ground_truth)

    binary_misuse = (ground_truth > 0.75).astype(int)
    if binary_misuse.sum() > 0 and binary_misuse.sum() < len(binary_misuse):
        auc_final = roc_auc_score(binary_misuse, final_scores)
        auc_best = roc_auc_score(binary_misuse, best_epoch_scores)
    else:
        auc_final = 0.5
        auc_best = 0.5

    print(f"  Final: Spearman={final_corr:.4f}, AUC={auc_final:.4f}")
    print(
        f"  Best epoch ({best_epoch_idx+1}): Spearman={best_epoch_corr:.4f}, AUC={auc_best:.4f}"
    )

    hf_dds = {}
    for i, row in df.iterrows():
        if row["is_hf"]:
            hf_dds[row["name"]] = {
                "DDS_final": float(final_scores[i]),
                "DDS_best": float(best_epoch_scores[i]),
                "semantic_drift": float(final_semantic[i]),
                "structural_drift": float(structural_drifts[i]),
                "misuse_label": float(row["misuse_label"]),
            }

    w_start = effective_weights_per_epoch[0]
    w_mid = effective_weights_per_epoch[len(effective_weights_per_epoch) // 2]
    w_end = effective_weights_per_epoch[-1]
    corr_start = float(spearmanr(w_start, misuse_vals)[0])
    corr_mid = float(spearmanr(w_mid, misuse_vals)[0])
    corr_end = float(spearmanr(w_end, misuse_vals)[0])

    all_results[strategy_name] = {
        "train_losses": train_losses,
        "train_corrs": train_corrs,
        "final_spearman": float(final_corr),
        "final_pvalue": float(final_pval),
        "best_spearman": float(best_epoch_corr),
        "best_epoch_idx": best_epoch_idx,
        "final_auc": float(auc_final),
        "best_auc": float(auc_best),
        "final_scores": final_scores.tolist(),
        "best_scores": best_epoch_scores.tolist(),
        "semantic_drifts": final_semantic.tolist(),
        "structural_drifts": structural_drifts.tolist(),
        "hf_dataset_dds": hf_dds,
        "dataset_names": df["name"].tolist(),
        "description": strategy_desc,
        "weight_corr_start": corr_start,
        "weight_corr_mid": corr_mid,
        "weight_corr_end": corr_end,
        "weights_start": w_start.tolist(),
        "weights_mid": w_mid.tolist(),
        "weights_end": w_end.tolist(),
    }

    experiment_data[strategy_name] = {
        "metrics": {"train": train_corrs},
        "losses": {"train": train_losses},
        "predictions": final_scores.tolist(),
        "ground_truth": ground_truth.tolist(),
        "drift_scores": final_scores.tolist(),
        "best_drift_scores": best_epoch_scores.tolist(),
        "semantic_drifts": final_semantic.tolist(),
        "structural_drifts": structural_drifts.tolist(),
        "spearman_correlation": float(final_corr),
        "pvalue": float(final_pval),
        "auc": float(auc_final),
        "dataset_names": df["name"].tolist(),
        "hf_dataset_dds": hf_dds,
    }

# ─── 8. Summary Table ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n=== SAMPLING STRATEGY SUMMARY ===")
print(
    f"{'Strategy':<20} {'Final ρ':<12} {'Best ρ':<12} {'Best AUC':<10} {'Best Epoch':<12} {'w-corr@end':<12}"
)
print("-" * 78)

        f"{res['best_auc']:<10.4f} {res['best_epoch_idx']+1:<12} {res['weight_corr_end']:<12.4f}"
    )
    if res["best_spearman"] > best_corr_overall:
        best_corr_overall = res["best_spearman"]
        best_strategy = strat_name

print(f"\nBest sampling strategy: {best_strategy} (Spearman={best_corr_overall:.4f})")

# ─── 9. HuggingFace DDS Report ────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n=== Documentation Drift Score (DDS) — HuggingFace Datasets ===")
best_res = all_results[best_strategy]
print(f"Strategy: {best_strategy}")
print(
    f"{'Dataset':<15} {'HF ID':<35} {'DDS':<8} {'Semantic':<10} {'Structural':<12} {'Misuse GT':<10}"

for ds_name, dds_info in best_res["hf_dataset_dds"].items():

        f"{dds_info['misuse_label']:<10.3f}"
    )

binary_misuse = (ground_truth > 0.75).astype(int)
best_scores_arr = np.array(best_res["best_scores"])
q75 = np.percentile(best_scores_arr, 75)
in_top_quartile = (best_scores_arr >= q75).astype(int)
tp = np.sum(binary_misuse & in_top_quartile)
recall_top_q = tp / max(binary_misuse.sum(), 1)
print(

)
print(f"AUC (best strategy): {best_res['best_auc']:.4f}")

# ─── 10. Weight Distribution Analysis ────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n=== Sampling Weight Distribution Analysis ===")
print(f"{'Strategy':<20} {'w-corr@start':<14} {'w-corr@mid':<14} {'w-corr@end':<14}")

for strat_name, res in all_results.items():

# ─── 11. Plots ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
colors_strat = {

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")
    experiment_data = {}

strategies = list(experiment_data.keys())
colors = {
    "uniform": "blue",
    "misuse_weighted": "green",
    "curriculum": "orange",
    "hf_oversampled": "red",
}

# Plot 1: Spearman correlation training curves
try:
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))
    for strat in strategies:
        corrs = experiment_data[strat]["metrics"]["train"]
        plt.plot(
            corrs, label=strat, color=colors.get(strat, "gray"), linewidth=2, alpha=0.85
        )
    plt.xlabel("Epoch")
    plt.ylabel("Spearman ρ")
    plt.title(
        "Training Spearman Correlation Over Epochs\nSampling Strategy Ablation — All Datasets (HuggingFace + Synthetic)"
    )
    plt.legend(fontsize=9)
    plt.grid(True)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_all_datasets_spearman_curves.png"), dpi=150
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved spearman curves plot")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating spearman curves plot: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 2: Training loss curves
try:
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))
    for strat in strategies:
        losses = experiment_data[strat]["losses"]["train"]
        plt.plot(
            losses,
            label=strat,
            color=colors.get(strat, "gray"),
            linewidth=2,
            alpha=0.85,
        )
    plt.xlabel("Epoch")
    plt.ylabel("Weighted MSE Loss")
    plt.title(
        "Training Loss Over Epochs\nSampling Strategy Ablation — All Datasets (HuggingFace + Synthetic)"
    )
    plt.legend(fontsize=9)
    plt.grid(True)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_all_datasets_loss_curves.png"), dpi=150
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved loss curves plot")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating loss curves plot: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 3: Final metric bar chart (Spearman + AUC per strategy)
try:
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
    x = np.arange(len(strategies))
    spearman_vals = [experiment_data[s]["spearman_correlation"] for s in strategies]
    auc_vals = [experiment_data[s]["auc"] for s in strategies]
    bars1 = ax.bar(
        x - 0.2,
        spearman_vals,
        0.35,
        label="Final Spearman ρ",
        color="steelblue",
        alpha=0.85,
    )
    bars2 = ax.bar(
        x + 0.2, auc_vals, 0.35, label="Final AUC", color="coral", alpha=0.85
    )
    for bar, val in zip(bars1, spearman_vals):
        ax.text(
            bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
            bar.get_height() + 0.01,
            f"{val:.3f}",
            ha="center",
            va="bottom",
            fontsize=9,
        )
    for bar, val in zip(bars2, auc_vals):
        ax.text(
            bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
            bar.get_height() + 0.01,
            f"{val:.3f}",
            ha="center",
            va="bottom",
            fontsize=9,
        )
    ax.set_xticks(x)
    ax.set_xticklabels([s.replace("_", "\n") for s in strategies], fontsize=9)
    ax.set_ylabel("Score")
    ax.set_ylim(0, 1.2)
    ax.set_title(
        "Final Spearman ρ and AUC by Sampling Strategy\nAll Datasets (HuggingFace + Synthetic)"
    )
    ax.legend()
    ax.grid(True, axis="y")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_all_datasets_final_metrics_bar.png"),
        dpi=150,
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved final metrics bar chart")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating final metrics bar chart: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 4: Per-dataset DDS scores vs ground truth for each strategy
try:
    dataset_names = experiment_data[strategies[0]]["dataset_names"]
    ground_truth = np.array(experiment_data[strategies[0]]["ground_truth"])
    n_ds = len(dataset_names)

        )

        x_ds,

    ax.set_xticklabels(dataset_names, rotation=45, ha="right", fontsize=7)
    ax.set_ylabel("DDS Score")
    ax.set_title(
        "Per-Dataset Documentation Drift Score (DDS) by Sampling Strategy\nvs Ground Truth Misuse Label — All Datasets (HuggingFace + Synthetic)"
    )
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    ax.grid(True, axis="y")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_all_datasets_per_dataset_dds.png"), dpi=150
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved per-dataset DDS plot")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating per-dataset DDS plot: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 5: Semantic vs Structural drift scatter per strategy
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(
        1, len(strategies), figsize=(16, 4), sharey=True, sharex=True
    )
    for idx, (strat, ax) in enumerate(zip(strategies, axes)):
        sem = np.array(experiment_data[strat]["semantic_drifts"])
        struct = np.array(experiment_data[strat]["structural_drifts"])
        gt = np.array(experiment_data[strat]["ground_truth"])
        sc = ax.scatter(
            sem,
            struct,
            c=gt,
            cmap="RdYlGn_r",
            vmin=0,
            vmax=1,
            s=60,
            edgecolors="k",
            linewidths=0.5,
        )
        for i, name in enumerate(experiment_data[strat]["dataset_names"]):
            ax.annotate(name, (sem[i], struct[i]), fontsize=5, alpha=0.7)
        ax.set_xlabel("Semantic Drift")
        ax.set_title(f"{strat}", fontsize=9)
        if idx == 0:
            ax.set_ylabel("Structural Drift")
    fig.colorbar(sc, ax=axes[-1], label="Ground Truth Misuse")
    fig.suptitle(
        "Semantic vs Structural Drift (color=Misuse Label)\nAll Datasets (HuggingFace + Synthetic)",
        fontsize=11,
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(
            working_dir, "ablation_all_datasets_semantic_vs_structural_scatter.png"
        ),
        dpi=150,
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved semantic vs structural scatter plot")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating scatter plot: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 6: HuggingFace dataset DDS breakdown across strategies
try:
    hf_names = list(experiment_data[strategies[0]]["hf_dataset_dds"].keys())
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, len(hf_names), figsize=(14, 5), sharey=True)
    metric_keys = ["semantic_drift", "structural_drift", "DDS_final"]
    metric_labels = ["Semantic Drift", "Structural Drift", "Combined DDS"]

        ]

            y=gt_val,

            linestyle="--",
            linewidth=1.8,
            label=f"GT={gt_val}" if ds_idx == 0 else "",
        )
        ax.set_xticks(x4)
        ax.set_xticklabels([s.replace("_", "\n") for s in strategies], fontsize=7)
        ax.set_title(f"{ds_name}", fontsize=9)
        ax.set_ylim(0, 1.1)
        ax.grid(True, axis="y")
        if ds_idx == 0:
            ax.set_ylabel("Score")
    handles, labels = axes[0].get_legend_handles_labels()
    fig.legend(handles, labels, loc="upper right", fontsize=8)
    fig.suptitle(
        "DDS Components by Sampling Strategy\nLeft to Right: HuggingFace Datasets (emotion, ag_news, imdb)",
        fontsize=11,
    )

        os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_huggingface_datasets_dds_breakdown.png"),
        dpi=150,

    print(f"Error creating HF DDS breakdown plot: {e}")

# Print summary metrics

for strat in strategies:
    print(
        f"{strat}: Spearman={experiment_data[strat]['spearman_correlation']:.4f}, AUC={experiment_data[strat]['auc']:.4f}"
    )
plot_plan: The experiment trains a DriftEncoder model with four different sampling strategies (uniform, misuse_weighted, curriculum, hf_oversampled) to detect documentation drift/misuse in NLP datasets. The data contains training metrics (Spearman correlation, loss), drift scores, ground truth misuse labels, and per-dataset breakdowns. I'll create visualizations covering: (1) training curves (Spearman ρ and loss) per strategy, (2) final metric comparisons (bar chart), (3) per-dataset DDS scores vs ground truth, (4) semantic vs structural drift scatter, (5) curriculum weight evolution approximation, and (6) HuggingFace dataset-specific DDS breakdown. Each plot will be saved separately with descriptive names.

plot_analyses.analysis: IMDB DDS components by strategy: semantic drift is moderate-high (~0.58–0.77) and structural drift is fairly stable (~0.62). Combined DDS is high for uniform (~0.71) and peaks for misuse_weighted (~0.81), crossing the misuse GT=0.8 threshold; however combined DDS collapses for curriculum and hf_oversampled (~0.08–0.10) despite similar component magnitudes, indicating the combination rule/normalization is behaving inconsistently across strategies (likely a gating/weighting bug or a sign flip). Misuse_weighted appears to align combined score with GT, while curriculum/hf_oversampled produce near-zero combined scores that are not credible given component values.

Emotion DDS components by strategy: structural drift is consistently high (~0.70) across all strategies; semantic drift increases from ~0.46 (uniform) to ~0.69 (hf_oversampled). Combined DDS is moderate for uniform (~0.51) and higher for misuse_weighted (~0.63), but again collapses for curriculum/hf_oversampled (~0.08–0.09) despite high component drifts. With GT=0.75, none of the combined scores reach the threshold; the same inconsistency suggests the combined DDS computation differs by strategy (e.g., curriculum/hf_oversampled might be outputting a different quantity such as a learned weight or residual).

Training Spearman correlation over epochs (all datasets): uniform and misuse_weighted start ~0.30 and gradually rise; misuse_weighted is higher early (peaks ~0.55 around epoch 5) and both surge after ~epoch 12, saturating near ~0.95 by ~16–18 epochs. Curriculum degrades over training, dropping from ~0.30 to negative (~-0.25) by ~epoch 10, indicating the curriculum schedule is anti-correlated with ground-truth ranking (likely emphasizing easy/low-misuse examples in a way that inverts the target). hf_oversampled stays flat around ~0.28–0.33 and stops early (~epoch 10), suggesting either early stopping or failure to learn a meaningful ranking under that sampling distribution.

Final Spearman ρ and AUC by sampling strategy: uniform and misuse_weighted tie at final Spearman ρ=0.957 and AUC=1.0, indicating near-perfect ranking and perfect classification performance in this setup; misuse_weighting does not improve final metrics but may improve convergence speed (as seen in the epoch curve). Curriculum has final Spearman ρ=-0.243 with AUC=0.300, clearly failing (worse than random). hf_oversampled is substantially worse than uniform/misuse_weighted (Spearman ρ=0.284, AUC=0.567), suggesting oversampling HF datasets introduces distribution shift or label imbalance that harms learning.

Per-dataset DDS vs ground truth misuse (all datasets): misuse_weighted generally yields the highest DDS bars across many datasets and tracks the ground-truth misuse curve better than uniform. Curriculum and hf_oversampled often produce much lower DDS (notably for high-misuse datasets like COMPAS/Adult_Income/ImageNet/CelebA), contradicting the expectation that high misuse should map to high DDS. For low-misuse datasets (MNIST/MovieLens), all strategies give lower DDS, but curriculum sometimes inflates (e.g., WikiText-103) relative to others, consistent with the negative Spearman result (misordered ranking). This plot reinforces that curriculum/hf_oversampled are miscalibrated or that their DDS outputs are on a different scale.

DDS components across HF datasets (emotion, ag_news, imdb): emotion shows combined DDS increasing with hf_oversampled (~0.69) and curriculum (~0.63) relative to uniform (~0.49), unlike earlier single-dataset emotion plot where curriculum/hf_oversampled collapsed—this discrepancy suggests the figures are generated from different runs or different combined-score definitions. ag_news shows curriculum and hf_oversampled producing high combined DDS (~0.81–0.85) above the GT line (~0.65), while uniform/misuse_weighted are ~0.51–0.52. imdb shows hf_oversampled highest combined (~0.76) approaching GT=0.8; curriculum is lowest (~0.58). Across these HF-only panels, combined DDS behaves sensibly (not near-zero), so the near-zero combined DDS in other plots likely indicates a bug in those specific computations/exports.

Training loss over epochs (all datasets): all strategies converge to very low loss (~0.01–0.03) by ~epoch 11–12, with small differences early. Curriculum starts with much lower loss initially (~0.41 vs ~0.54–0.60) and remains lower through ~epoch 7, yet yields poor Spearman/AUC—suggesting the loss being optimized is not aligned with the ranking/ground-truth objective under curriculum sampling (e.g., it may overfit to easy examples or distort the effective target distribution). Misuse_weighted has the highest early loss but catches up; final losses are similar, so performance differences are not due to optimization failure but objective/sampling mismatch.

AG_NEWS DDS components by strategy: semantic drift rises strongly for curriculum/hf_oversampled (~0.81–0.86) while structural drift stays high (~0.74). Combined DDS is moderate for uniform (~0.56) and highest for misuse_weighted (~0.68), slightly above the GT=0.65 threshold; again combined DDS collapses for curriculum/hf_oversampled (~0.11), inconsistent with high component drifts. This mirrors the inconsistency seen in imdb/emotion single-dataset plots and suggests a systematic issue in how combined DDS is computed/logged for curriculum/hf_oversampled in these per-dataset component plots.

Composite ablation dashboard: Spearman curve and best-metric bar chart replicate earlier findings—uniform and misuse_weighted reach best Spearman ~0.957 and AUC=1.0; curriculum best Spearman ~0.297 with AUC~0.567 (note: differs from the 'final' plot where curriculum was negative, implying best-vs-final distinction or inconsistent logging); hf_oversampled best Spearman ~0.375 and AUC~0.633. Sampling-weights heatmap shows hf_oversampled heavily upweights a subset of HF datasets (emotion/ag_news/imdb) relative to others; misuse_weighted moderately upweights several datasets; curriculum weights are closer to uniform with mild variation. Curriculum weight evolution plot shows low-misuse datasets get decreasing weight over epochs while high-misuse datasets get increasing weight, which should help—yet overall curriculum performance is poor, implying the curriculum schedule may be reversed in the actual training run used for Spearman, or the grouping/thresholding for high vs low misuse is mismatched.

Semantic vs structural drift scatter (colored by misuse label): across strategies, high-misuse datasets (red) tend to cluster at high structural drift (~1.0) and moderate-high semantic drift (~0.4–0.85), suggesting structural drift is a strong discriminator but may saturate (ceiling effect). Some datasets (imdb) show high semantic but lower structural (~0.62), and others (WikiText-103) show low semantic but very high structural, indicating the two components capture different failure modes. The point clouds look similar across strategies, implying sampling affects learned combination/ranking more than the underlying per-dataset component estimates, or that component estimates are computed independently of sampling.

vlm_feedback_summary: Across plots, uniform and misuse_weighted sampling reliably achieve near-perfect alignment with ground-truth misuse (Spearman ~0.96, AUC=1.0), with misuse_weighted improving early-epoch correlation but not final performance. Curriculum sampling consistently fails (Spearman becomes negative; AUC ~0.3–0.57 depending on best vs final), and hf_oversampled underperforms (Spearman ~0.28–0.38; AUC ~0.57–0.63). A major red flag is that several per-dataset component plots show Combined DDS collapsing to ~0.1 for curriculum/hf_oversampled despite high semantic/structural drift components—contradicted by other HF-only component panels where Combined DDS is reasonable—strongly suggesting an implementation/logging inconsistency in the combined-score computation for those strategies. Structural drift appears high and sometimes saturated for high-misuse datasets, while semantic drift provides additional separation for certain datasets (e.g., imdb). Loss curves converge similarly across strategies, so failures are due to sampling/objective misalignment rather than optimization instability.

overall_plan:
Ablation name: Usage Text Representation Strategy Ablation.
The ablation study tests 5 usage text representation strategies (curated_only, raw_only, keyword_extracted, sliding_window, combined_baseline) by building different usage profiles for each dataset and training a DriftEncoder to measure how sensitive drift detection is to usage text construction. For each strategy, we build usage texts differently, fit a TF-IDF vectorizer, train the encoder with cosine similarity loss, compute Spearman correlation and AUC against ground truth misuse labels, and track per-HF-dataset DDS scores. Results are saved as numpy arrays and visualized across 6 subplots plus per-strategy detail plots. The experiment_data dictionary stores all metrics following the required naming convention.

metric_names.description: Best Spearman rank correlation coefficient between predicted and ground truth drift/misuse scores across strategies.
Best Area Under the ROC Curve for misuse classification across strategies.
Mean Squared Error training loss across strategies.
Mean semantic drift score across all HuggingFace datasets per strategy, measuring semantic divergence between intended and actual usage.
Mean structural drift score across all HuggingFace datasets per strategy, measuring structural divergence between intended and actual usage.
Best Dataset Drift Score for the 'emotion' HuggingFace dataset across strategies.
Best Dataset Drift Score for the 'ag_news' HuggingFace dataset across strategies.
Best Dataset Drift Score for the 'imdb' HuggingFace dataset across strategies.

# ─── 2. Usage Scenarios ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
usage_scenarios = {

}

# ─── 3. Structured Schema for Drift Detection ───────────────────────────────
schema_dimensions = {
    "task": [
        "classification",
        "detection",
        "generation",
        "regression",
        "qa",
        "segmentation",
        "translation",

        "social media",

        "english",

        "non-english",
    ],

        "sentiment",
        "emotion",
        "topic",
        "binary",
        "multiclass",
        "fine-grained",
        "zero-shot",
        "transfer",
    ],
    "scope": [
        "benchmark",
        "production",
        "research",
        "real-time",
        "cross-domain",
        "out-of-distribution",
        "in-distribution",
    ],
}


def extract_schema_vector(text):
    text_lower = text.lower()
    vec = []
    for dim, keywords in schema_dimensions.items():
        for kw in keywords:
            vec.append(1.0 if kw in text_lower else 0.0)
    return np.array(vec, dtype=float)


def compute_structural_drift(doc_text, usage_text):
    dv = extract_schema_vector(doc_text)
    uv = extract_schema_vector(usage_text)
    union = np.maximum(dv, uv).sum()
    if union == 0:
        return 0.5
    intersection = np.minimum(dv, uv).sum()
    jaccard_sim = intersection / union
    novel_usage = np.sum((uv > 0) & (dv == 0))
    total_usage_dims = max(uv.sum(), 1)
    novelty_penalty = novel_usage / total_usage_dims
    return 0.6 * (1 - jaccard_sim) + 0.4 * novelty_penalty

    },

# ─── 5. Usage Text Representation Strategies ────────────────────────────────
print("\n=== Usage Text Representation Strategy Ablation ===")


def extract_tfidf_keywords(text, n_keywords=30):
    words = text.lower().split()
    words = [w.strip(".,!?;:()[]\"'") for w in words if len(w) > 3]
    freq = {}
    for w in words:
        freq[w] = freq.get(w, 0) + 1
    sorted_words = sorted(freq.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
    top_keywords = [w for w, f in sorted_words[:n_keywords]]
    return " ".join(top_keywords)


def build_sliding_window_usage(texts, window_size=10, n_windows=5):
    if len(texts) < window_size:
        return " ".join(texts)
    step = max(1, (len(texts) - window_size) // max(n_windows - 1, 1))
    windows = []
    for start in range(0, len(texts) - window_size + 1, step):
        window_texts = texts[start : start + window_size]
        windows.append(" ".join(window_texts))
        if len(windows) >= n_windows:
            break
    return " | ".join(windows[:n_windows])
